message:
fix(scheduler): use fully-qualified tracing_compat::warn! macro path in ThreeLaneScheduler

The global-queue-limit warning in ThreeLaneScheduler::schedule_task()
was using the short `crate::warn!` macro path which no longer resolves
correctly after the tracing compatibility layer was reorganized. This
updates it to the fully-qualified `crate::tracing_compat::warn!` path,
consistent with how all other tracing macros are invoked throughout the
scheduler module.

Without this fix, the warn! call fails to compile when the crate-level
re-export is removed, silently dropping the diagnostic that fires when
the global ready queue hits its configured capacity limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
